title: carex nigra subsp. distachya
intro: Carex nigra subsp. distachya, also known as black sedge, is a subspecies of perennial sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Carex nigra subsp. distachya include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Carex nigra subsp. distachya are typically made through infusions or decoctions of the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
